commands_and_summary:
Call to tool write_to_file with arguments {"filename": "Dockerfile", "text": "FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential dependencies including gnupg for repository management
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gnupg git wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq

# Add necessary repositories
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 \
 && apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88 \
 && echo 'deb https://hyperledger.jfrog.io/artifactory/indy focal dev' >> /etc/apt/sources.list

# Update and install essential dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip python3-nacl libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev zstd ruby ruby-dev rubygems gcc make libssl1.0.0 ursa=0.3.2-1 libindy=1.15.0~1625-bionic

# Clean apt caches
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone project
ARG REPO_URL=https://github.com/hyperledger-indy/indy-node
ARG PROJECT_DIR=indy-node
RUN git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}"}

commands_and_summary:
Call to tool write_to_file with arguments {"filename": "Dockerfile", "text": "FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gnupg git wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq

# Add Hyperledger repository and keys
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 \
    && apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88 \
    && echo 'deb https://hyperledger.jfrog.io/artifactory/indy focal dev' >> /etc/apt/sources.list

# Update repositories and install packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip python3-nacl libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev zstd ruby ruby-dev rubygems gcc make libssl1.0.0 ursa=0.3.2-1 libindy=1.15.0~1625-bionic

# Clean apt caches
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone project repository
ARG REPO_URL=https://github.com/hyperledger-indy/indy-node
ARG PROJECT_DIR=indy-node
RUN git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}"}

written_files:
FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential dependencies including gnupg for repository management
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gnupg git wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq

# Add necessary repositories
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 \
 && apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88 \
 && echo 'deb https://hyperledger.jfrog.io/artifactory/indy focal dev' >> /etc/apt/sources.list

# Update and install essential dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip python3-nacl libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev zstd ruby ruby-dev rubygems gcc make libssl1.0.0 ursa=0.3.2-1 libindy=1.15.0~1625-bionic

# Clean apt caches
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone project
ARG REPO_URL=https://github.com/hyperledger-indy/indy-node
ARG PROJECT_DIR=indy-node
RUN git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}

FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential dependencies including gnupg for repository management
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gnupg git wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq

# Add necessary repositories
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 \
 && apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88 \
 && echo 'deb https://hyperledger.jfrog.io/artifactory/indy focal dev' >> /etc/apt/sources.list

# Update and install essential dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip python3-nacl libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev zstd ruby ruby-dev rubygems gcc make libssl1.1 ursa libindy

# Clean apt caches
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone project
ARG REPO_URL=https://github.com/hyperledger-indy/indy-node
ARG PROJECT_DIR=indy-node
RUN git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}

FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gnupg git wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq

# Add Hyperledger repository and keys
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 \
    && apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88 \
    && echo 'deb https://hyperledger.jfrog.io/artifactory/indy focal dev' >> /etc/apt/sources.list

# Update repositories and install packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip python3-nacl libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev zstd ruby ruby-dev rubygems gcc make libssl1.0.0 ursa=0.3.2-1 libindy=1.15.0~1625-bionic

# Clean apt caches
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone project repository
ARG REPO_URL=https://github.com/hyperledger-indy/indy-node
ARG PROJECT_DIR=indy-node
RUN git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}

previous_attempts.problems: The setup encountered multiple failures primarily due to missing or unresolvable dependencies. Key errors include: 1. At Step 1, the Docker build failed with a message indicating that packages like 'rocksdb', 'libssl1.0.0', 'ursa', and 'libindy' could not be located, leading to a non-zero exit code. 2. At Step 3, an error occurred due to tilde (~) expansion not being supported in the environment, suggesting that absolute paths were needed. 3. Step 6 revealed issues with repositories related to missing GPG keys and certificate verification failures for 'sovrin.org' and 'hyperledger.jfrog.io'. 4. Steps 16 and 20 failed with errors indicating that the installation of 'libssl1.0.0', 'ursa', and 'rocksdb' could not be completed as these packages were missing in the current environment.
previous_attempts.actions: 1. Created a Dockerfile based on 'ubuntu:20.04' to set up the environment. 2. Initially attempted to install required packages, including system dependencies and specific versions of libraries. 3. The first Docker build failed due to unresolved package dependencies. 4. Adjusted the Dockerfile, removing certain packages in subsequent attempts. 5. Added Hyperledger and Sovrin repositories to sources.list to try to resolve missing packages. 6. Attempted to update GPG keys to handle package signing issues. 7. Continued failing attempts to run 'apt-get update' and 'apt-get install', ultimately leading to missing packages and unresolved repository configurations.
previous_attempts.progress: 40% complete. The Docker image was built successfully, and the container started. However, the installation of critical dependencies failed, leaving the environment incomplete. The focus has shifted to resolving package installation issues, which are blocking progress. The environment is partially set up but not ready for testing.
previous_attempts.lessons: 1. The project requires specific dependencies such as 'libssl1.0.0', 'rocksdb', 'ursa', and 'libindy', which may not be readily available in the default repositories of Ubuntu 20.04. 2. Issues with GPG keys and repository configurations must be addressed prior to package installations. 3. Tilde (~) expansion in package versions is not supported; absolute paths or alternative version specifications should be used. 4. The Docker environment must ensure that all required repositories are accessible and correctly configured.
previous_attempts.suggestions: 1. Update the Dockerfile to only include necessary dependencies and check the availability of each package in the repositories specified. 2. Modify the package installation commands to avoid using unsupported features like tilde expansion. 3. Verify the inclusion of all relevant GPG keys and ensure that repositories are accessible and valid before running 'apt-get update'. 4. Consider using alternate package sources or versions if certain packages are permanently unavailable. 5. Execute the following commands: 'apt-get update -y' followed by 'apt-get install -y <list_of_packages>' to systematically address failures.
previous_attempts.dockerfile_recommendation:
FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git wget gnupg apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq python3 python3-pip python3-nacl libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev zstd ruby ruby-dev rubygems gcc make

# Clean apt caches
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Add necessary repositories
RUN echo 'deb https://hyperledger.jfrog.io/artifactory/indy focal dev' >> /etc/apt/sources.list

# Add GPG key for Hyperledger
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61

# Update and install dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3-nacl rocksdb libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev zstd ruby ruby-dev rubygems gcc make libssl1.0.0 ursa libindy

# Clone project
ARG REPO_URL=https://github.com/hyperledger-indy/indy-node
ARG PROJECT_DIR=indy-node
RUN git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}
previous_attempts.dockerfile_used:
FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install git separately first
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y git

# Install other system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    wget gnupg apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq \
    python3 python3-pip python3-nacl \
    libgflags-dev libsnappy-dev zlib1g-dev libbz2-dev liblz4-dev \
    zstd ruby ruby-dev rubygems gcc make

# Clean apt caches
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone project (handled by Dockerfile template)
ARG REPO_URL=https://github.com/hyperledger-indy/indy-node
ARG PROJECT_DIR=indy-node
RUN test -n "$REPO_URL" && git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}

previous_attempts.problems: Multiple issues were encountered during the Docker build and package installation processes. Notably, package installations failed due to missing dependencies and incorrect package names. For instance, the build failed with missing 'gnupg' during Step 4, which is essential for managing repository keys. Subsequent failures were seen with fetching packages leading to SSL certificate issues and '404 Not Found' errors when attempting to access repositories during Steps 5, 7, and 8. As a result, numerous required packages such as 'libssl1.0.0', 'rocksdb', 'ursa', and 'libindy' could not be located as indicated in Steps 10 and 18. The agent got stuck primarily at Steps 3 to 20, where it repeatedly attempted to write to the Dockerfile and build the image without success, leading to a more complex debugging situation. The root causes of these failures include missing dependencies, incorrect package versions, and repository access issues, particularly with the Sovrin repository.
previous_attempts.actions: The first action was to create a Dockerfile based on Ubuntu 20.04, which included essential dependency installations. Initial attempts to build the Docker image failed, revealing the absence of 'gnupg'. The Dockerfile was revised multiple times to include the necessary repository configurations and dependencies, but failures persisted due to SSL certificate verification issues and missing packages. Attempts to install dependencies were made directly using shell commands during the build process, which led to partial success but ultimately failed to resolve all dependencies. The approach evolved from simply trying to build the Docker image to modifying the sources list and adjusting package installations to resolve conflicts. Various packages were attempted to be fetched from both Hyperledger and Sovrin repositories, but consistent failures indicated unresolved issues with the repositories themselves and package availability.
previous_attempts.progress: 40% complete. The Docker image was successfully built (Step 9) and a container was started, allowing for some level of interaction. However, many required dependencies were either not installed or could not be located. The agent's inability to successfully install all dependencies indicates that the environment is not fully prepared for running tests, with critical packages still missing.
previous_attempts.lessons: Key dependencies such as 'libssl1.0.0', 'rocksdb', 'ursa', and 'libindy' were identified as essential but problematic due to versioning and repository issues. The configuration of the sources list proved crucial, particularly the need to manage GPG keys and repository access properly. The project appears to have specific requirements for certain versions of libraries, which need to be clearly defined in the Dockerfile to avoid conflicts. The command structure for building the Docker image needs to be adjusted to handle dependencies in a way that prevents failures related to package access and installation.
previous_attempts.suggestions: 1. Modify the Dockerfile to ensure 'gnupg' is installed before attempting to add repository keys. 2. Ensure that the sources list points to valid repositories and remove any that lead to access issues. 3. Use 'libssl1.1' instead of 'libssl1.0.0' if it is available, as it is more likely to be supported. 4. Test package availability separately using 'apt-cache policy <package_name>' before attempting to install. 5. Verify the SSL certificates of the repositories or consider switching to more reliable sources if needed. 6. Confirm the correct package names and versions in the Dockerfile to avoid 404 errors during installation.

FROM ubuntu:20.04

ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
ENV TZ=Europe/Berlin

# Install essential dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y gnupg && apt-get install -y git wget apt-transport-https ca-certificates apt-utils curl jq

# Add necessary repositories
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 9692C00E657DDE61 \
 && apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys CE7709D068DB5E88 \
 && echo 'deb https://hyperledger.jfrog.io/artifactory/indy focal dev' >> /etc/apt/sources.list

# Update and install essential dependencies (removing bionic-security entry)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip python3-nacl ruby ruby-dev

# Clean apt caches
RUN apt-get -y autoremove && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Clone project
ARG REPO_URL=https://github.com/hyperledger-indy/indy-node
ARG PROJECT_DIR=indy-node
RUN git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" "$PROJECT_DIR"

WORKDIR /app/${PROJECT_DIR}
